title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-14
summary: Good morning, afternoon, or evening, depending on where youre tuning in from! And a very warm welcome to The Hungarian Daily, your essential podcast for foreign professionals, expats, digital nomads, and international business executives navigating life and work here in Budapest, Hungary.It is Monday, April 13, 2026, and what a seismic shift we have to discuss today. If youre just waking up, or perhaps still reeling from the weekends events, buckle up. Hungary has just witnessed a political earthquake of historic proportions, and the tremors are already being felt across the economy and beyond.Our top story, dominating every headline and conversation, is the **landslide victory of Péter Magyar** (Pay-ter Mah-djar) and his Tisza (Tih-sah) Party in yesterday’s parliamentary elections. This outcome has swept away the Orbán (Or-bahn) era, bringing to an end Viktor Orbáns remarkable 16-year tenure as Prime Minister. Its a moment that many thought might never come, and the capital, Budapest (Boo-da-pesht), erupted into spontaneous celebrations last night, with Reuters reporting that the city truly turned into a party zone as the news broke.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-15
summary: Jó reggelt, Budapest! And a very warm welcome to The Hungarian Daily, your essential guide to news and insights for foreign professionals, expats, digital nomads, and international business executives living and working right here in Hungary. Im your host, and Im thrilled to have you join us this morning, Wednesday, April 15, 2026.Weve got a packed agenda today, diving into everything from the evolving political landscape to key economic indicators and cultural happenings that shape your life in this vibrant country. While all our top stories today carry a similar urgency score, rest assured, weve meticulously curated them to bring you the most relevant and impactful information, with a special emphasis on how these developments resonate with our international community.Lets kick things off with a look at the economic pulse of Hungary, as this directly impacts your investments, your cost of living, and the overall business environment.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-18
summary: An EU delegation is set to meet Hungarys incoming government amid Polish hopes for a policy shift on Ukraine aid, while the Forint strengthens to a multi-year high post-election.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-19
summary: Todays Hungarian Daily highlights Péter Magyars high-level talks with Brussels to unblock billions in EU funds, signaling a potential economic and political turning point for Hungary, with a new government aiming to fix the economy. We also cover Budapest Airports new terminal development, the future of the Visegrad Four, and a brief note on Russian Foreign Minister Lavrovs comments, alongside our essential Currency Corner and a look at quirky social media trends.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-20
summary: Today, Monday, April 20, 2026, The Hungarian Daily focuses on the new Tisza governments potential to unlock billions in frozen EU funds, a critical development for Hungarys economy and international standing, alongside a strengthening Forint and rising real wages. We also discuss energy security, Norways support for democratic reforms, and local expat queries for planning May 1st activities and bachelor parties in Budapest.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-21
summary: Todays Hungarian Daily reports on the formation of the new Tisza government, with Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar announcing the first seven ministers and a sixteen-ministry cabinet, sparking discussions about economic reform and EU relations. We also cover positive news on net earnings outpacing inflation, the evolving dynamics of the Visegrad Four alliance, and Greenpeaces findings of no toxic pollution around Hungarys battery plants, before diving into expat-relevant social media topics on Budapest parking rules and finding specific imported goods.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-23
summary: Today on The Hungarian Daily, we explore a stunning new poll indicating a potential decline for Orbáns Fidesz party and the rise of Péter Magyars Tisza party, signaling a major political realignment in Hungary. We also cover the formation of the new government, key cabinet appointments, the future of education and Erasmus, and the Forints stability amidst global pressures, alongside positive shifts for Korean battery firms in Hungary.

summary: Todays Hungarian Daily covers the critical economic challenges facing Hungarys new government, the positive business shifts for Korean battery firms, and the significant political rally in Budapest against Orbán ahead of the vote. We also discuss tax plans, economic confidence, Orbáns potential parliamentary exit, and the future of the V4 group, along with essential currency and weather briefings for expats.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-27
summary: Today, Monday, April 27, 2026, The Hungarian Daily covered the EUs proposed industrial investment rules drawing Chinese criticism, Hungarys new economy minister Kapitány promising budget transparency amidst economic challenges, and a massive rally in Budapest urging the vote against Prime Minister Orbán. We also discussed U.S. Senator JD Vances support for Orbán, the potential future of the V4 group and EU decision-making without Orbáns veto, and updates on the Budapest-Belgrade railway, domestic political appointments, and Mayor Karácsonys urban renewal vision, alongside expat social media trends.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-28
summary: Todays The Hungarian Daily covers Hungarys pivotal moment as the new government prepares for high-stakes discussions in Brussels on Wednesday to unlock crucial EU funds, with Budapest anticipating 700 billion Forints for urban development. The broadcast also delves into the post-election political landscape, including the Tisza Partys stance and former PM Orbáns decision not to take his mandate, while examining the challenges and opportunities for Hungarys economy and its relationship with the EU. Additionally, it touches on Tiszas ambitious healthcare reform plans, the impending launch of the Belgrade-Budapest passenger rail, and a look at current social media trends among expats.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-29
summary: Todays broadcast covers a potential thaw in Hungary-EU relations, how Budapest companies are embracing workplace flexibility, and Wizz Airs plans for a major summer expansion.

title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-30
summary: Todays Hungarian Daily focuses on the pivotal political and economic shifts underway in Hungary, driven by the upcoming elections, the potential unlocking of crucial EU funds, and the emergence of new political forces. We delve into European calls for credit of trust for a new government, a reported pivot by Hungarys business elite, and the pressing question of economic recovery, all against a backdrop of significant public rallies and international interest.
